mcp_awareness.overview: This project has 9 configured MCP (Model Context Protocol) servers providing specialized capabilities. You MUST proactively use these tools when they align with your task to maximize output quality and efficiency. NEVER assume you cannot do something - check if an MCP tool can help first.

protocol.rule: ALWAYS check MCP tool availability before stating limitations
Use MCP tools PROACTIVELY, not just when explicitly asked
Prefer MCP tools over manual/simulated approaches when available
Document which MCP tools you used in your implementation notes
If an MCP tool fails, report the error and suggest alternatives

server.name: Ref
server.description: Search and read current documentation from official sources (GitHub, framework docs)

tool.name: ref_search_documentation
tool.usage: Search for documentation on frameworks, libraries, APIs
tool.when_to_use: - Before implementing ANY framework feature you're uncertain about - When API signatures or patterns may have changed since training cutoff - Researching best practices for Next.js, Flutter, React, etc. - Verifying current library versions and breaking changes

tool.name: ref_read_url
tool.usage: Read full documentation page content as markdown
tool.when_to_use: - After ref_search_documentation returns relevant URLs - To read complete API documentation before implementation - To verify exact code examples from official docs

server.relevant_agents: ALL - especially Dev, Architect, Test Architect
server.priority: CRITICAL - Use BEFORE implementing uncertain features

server.name: supabase
server.description: Direct Supabase database interaction for this project

tool.name: list_projects
tool.when_to_use: Understanding project configuration, verifying database connection

tool.name: list_tables
tool.usage: List all tables in the Supabase database
tool.when_to_use: Schema exploration, understanding data model, planning queries

tool.name: list_migrations
tool.when_to_use: Understanding schema evolution, debugging migration issues

tool.name: apply_migration
tool.when_to_use: Deploying schema changes, updating database structure

tool.name: execute_sql
tool.usage: Execute SQL queries directly against the database
tool.when_to_use: - Verifying data integrity during implementation - Testing queries before writing ORM code - Debugging data issues - Generating test fixtures - Validating acceptance criteria related to data

server.relevant_agents: Dev, Architect, Test Architect, Business Analyst
server.priority: HIGH - Use for database verification and data validation tasks

server.name: chrome-devtools
server.description: Browser automation and testing via Chrome DevTools Protocol

tool.name: navigate_page
tool.when_to_use: Loading web application for testing

tool.name: take_screenshot
tool.usage: Capture screenshot of current page state
tool.when_to_use: - Visual regression testing - Documenting UI implementation - Bug reporting with visual evidence - Validating responsive layouts

tool.name: take_snapshot
tool.when_to_use: Analyzing page structure, debugging rendering

tool.name: click
tool.when_to_use: Simulating user interactions during testing

tool.name: fill_form
tool.when_to_use: Testing form submission workflows

tool.name: wait_for
tool.when_to_use: Handling async operations in tests

tool.name: list_console_messages
tool.when_to_use: - Debugging JavaScript errors - Verifying logging implementation - Catching client-side issues

server.relevant_agents: Dev (web testing), Test Architect, UX Designer (verification)
server.priority: HIGH - Use for web UI validation and visual testing

server.name: playwright

tool.name: Various Playwright commands
tool.usage: Execute Playwright test scripts, generate tests, debug
tool.when_to_use: - Writing E2E tests for web application - Running automated test suites - Generating test code from user interactions - Cross-browser testing

server.priority: HIGH - Primary tool for web E2E testing

server.name: maestro
server.description: Mobile app testing framework for Flutter/iOS/Android

tool.name: Various Maestro commands
tool.usage: Execute mobile E2E tests, record flows, debug mobile UI
tool.when_to_use: - Writing E2E tests for Flutter mobile app - Testing native iOS/Android functionality - Validating mobile-specific workflows (camera, offline sync, etc.) - Recording user flows for test automation

server.priority: HIGH - Primary tool for mobile E2E testing

server.name: shadcn
server.description: shadcn/ui component library integration for Next.js web app

tool.name: Various shadcn commands
tool.usage: Add/manage shadcn UI components, generate component code
tool.when_to_use: - Adding new UI components to Next.js web app - Following design system standards - Ensuring accessible, consistent UI patterns - Accelerating frontend development

server.priority: MEDIUM - Use for web UI component implementation

server.name: vercel
server.description: Vercel deployment and hosting platform integration

tool.name: Various Vercel commands
tool.when_to_use: - Deploying Next.js web application - Managing preview/production environments - Configuring environment variables - Monitoring deployment status

server.priority: MEDIUM - Use for deployment and hosting tasks

server.name: ide
server.description: IDE integration for diagnostics and code analysis

tool.name: getDiagnostics
tool.when_to_use: - Identifying compilation errors before running builds - Validating code quality during implementation - Quick error checking without full test runs

server.priority: MEDIUM - Use for quick error checking

server.name: next-devtools
server.description: Next.js DevTools MCP server for inspecting and debugging Next.js applications

tool.name: Various Next.js DevTools commands
tool.usage: Inspect Next.js app structure, routes, components, and configuration
tool.when_to_use: - Understanding Next.js application architecture - Debugging routing issues - Analyzing component structure and dependencies - Inspecting Next.js configuration and build setup - Understanding App Router vs Pages Router structure - Analyzing server/client component boundaries - Debugging performance issues in Next.js apps

server.priority: HIGH - Use for Next.js application development and debugging

agent_role.name: Developer
agent_role.workflow_integration: 1. Before implementing: Use Ref tools to verify current docs 2. During implementation: Use IDE diagnostics for quick checks; use Next.js DevTools to inspect app structure 3. After implementation: Use Chrome DevTools or Maestro for UI validation 4. For data operations: Use Supabase tools to verify queries/schema 5. For Next.js features: Use next-devtools to understand routing, components, and configuration 6. Document all MCP tools used in implementation notes

agent_role.name: Test Architect
agent_role.workflow_integration: 1. Use Playwright for web E2E test implementation 2. Use Maestro for mobile E2E test implementation 3. Use Chrome DevTools for visual regression and debugging 4. Use Supabase tools for test fixture creation and data validation 5. Use Ref tools to verify testing framework best practices

agent_role.name: Architect
agent_role.workflow_integration: 1. Use Ref tools to research current architectural patterns 2. Use Supabase tools to understand existing data model 3. Use shadcn for UI architecture decisions 4. Use next-devtools to analyze Next.js app structure and routing patterns 5. Document MCP-based research in architecture decisions

agent_role.name: UX Designer
agent_role.workflow_integration: 1. Use Chrome DevTools to capture current UI state 2. Use shadcn to align with component library 3. Use Ref tools for accessibility and UX research

agent_role.name: Business Analyst
agent_role.workflow_integration: 1. Use Supabase tools to analyze current data model 2. Use SQL execution for data-driven requirements validation

agent_role.name: Scrum Master
agent_role.workflow_integration: 1. Use Vercel tools to check deployment status 2. Use Chrome DevTools for quick smoke tests

agent_role.name: Product Manager
agent_role.workflow_integration: 1. Use Chrome DevTools to document feature implementations 2. Use Supabase for data-driven product insights

failure_protocol.rule: If an MCP tool returns an error, report it clearly to the user
If authentication fails, inform user to check .mcp.json configuration
If a tool is unavailable, suggest alternative approaches
NEVER silently skip MCP tools - always announce when you're using them

mcp_awareness.documentation_requirement:
When using MCP tools during implementation or testing, document in your output: - Which MCP tools were used - What queries/commands were executed - What results/insights were obtained - How the MCP tool output informed your decisions
Example: ```markdown ### MCP Tools Used - **mcp__Ref__ref_search_documentation**: Searched "Next.js 16.0 server actions" - **mcp__Ref__ref_read_url**: Read official Next.js docs on form actions - **mcp__supabase__execute_sql**: Verified inspection_template table schema - **mcp__chrome-devtools__take_screenshot**: Captured form rendering for validation ```
